task_completion.description: Create a Graphite dashboard named 'NOC Legacy View'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the dashboard title 'NOC Legacy View' at the top of the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'CPU Utilization (Cacti)' using the cactiStyle function on the specified wildcard metric
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'CPU Utilization (Cacti)'. The query or legend should show 'cactiStyle(servers.ec2_instance_*.cpu.*)' and the graph should display Cacti-like formatting (e.g., specific legend alignment, current/max/min values).

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'Traffic & I/O (Styled)' with specific line colors for network and disk metrics
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'Traffic & I/O (Styled)'. The graph lines and legend must show 'servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in' colored green and 'servers.ec2_instance_1.disk.write_bytes' colored blue.

task_completion.description: Add a graph titled 'Temperature with Baseline' showing machine temperature with lineWidth 3 and an average aggregateLine
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a graph panel titled 'Temperature with Baseline'. The graph should show 'servers.datacenter.machine_temperature' with a noticeably thick line (lineWidth 3) and a horizontal straight line representing the average baseline (aggregateLine).
Look for a 'Dashboard Saved' notification, a save confirmation dialog, or the dashboard name 'NOC Legacy View' appearing in the URL bar or saved dashboards list.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the Graphite web UI (browser window) being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The graphs are actually rendered with data
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph panels should display actual plotted lines representing the time-series data, not just empty panels or text descriptions.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected dashboard name and graph titles must appear within the Graphite UI components (headers, panel titles), not in a separate text file or notepad.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'NOC Legacy View'. The three expected graph titles are exactly 'CPU Utilization (Cacti)', 'Traffic & I/O (Styled)', and 'Temperature with Baseline'. The task uses real-world time-series data from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB), so the graphs should show realistic fluctuations for CPU, disk, network, and temperature metrics.
